verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is explicitly characterized as a soluble (non-transmembrane) protein, in contrast to IZUMO1/2/3 which are transmembrane proteins. Multiple independent sources designate it as an intra-acrosomal protein in sperm; its reduction after the acrosome reaction is consistent with luminal/soluble release rather than surface exposure. The Human Protein Atlas reports cytoplasmic localization; GeneCards/Alliance of Genome Resources places it in the nucleus. AlphaFold-Multimer modeling of the fertilization synapse confirms IZUMO4 does not integrate into the surface fusion complex. No TM domain, GPI anchor, or stable PM-anchoring mechanism has been described. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic program was found. The protein body has no documented outer-leaflet plasma membrane presence by any mechanism.
